what_it_does: Records ledger metadata for a design-source image asset named deck1-2026/p-12 and labels it as source design ground-truth.
observations: The file is a YAML-frontmatter ledger entry for an image/jpeg asset
It references a blob://b2 URI with a hash
Declared width and height are both 0
The body contains the line 'SOURCE design ground-truth — deck1-2026/p-12.jpg'
The links array is empty and there are no code symbols
standouts: Width and height are 0 despite being an image record
Blob reference includes a content hash
concerns: Image dimensions are reported as 0, which is unusual for an image asset
No links or symbols are provided, limiting internal traceability
notes: A ledger stub for a design deck image page that carries a ground-truth source label but lacks image dimensions and cross-references.
questions: Why are the width and height values zero for an image asset?
What content is represented by deck1-2026/p-12?
How is the blob hash verified or associated with the image bytes?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to catalog a source design ground-truth image; the file does that, but the zero dimensions and empty links make the metadata incomplete.
intention_signals: To register an authoritative source image from a design deck so it can be located, attributed, and reused downstream.
novelty: The explicit 'SOURCE design ground-truth' provenance label on an image ledger entry.
contribution: Provides a ground-truth image reference with a blob identifier, channel, path, timestamp, and author session that a fused system can use to locate and attribute the original design asset.
summary_for_embedding: Ledger metadata record for a JPEG image asset named deck1-2026/p-12 in the design-source channel, marked as source design ground-truth, with a blob hash and zero declared dimensions.
intention_for_embedding: To catalog and preserve an authoritative design-source image page for downstream retrieval and provenance.
